stem: What is the maximum value of \( ([[a]])\sin x + ([[b]])\cos x \)?
solution: Rewrite \( a\sin x + b\cos x = R\sin(x+\varphi) \) with \( R = \sqrt{a^2+b^2} \). Since \( \sin(x+\varphi) \) ranges over \( [-1,1] \), the maximum is \( R = \sqrt{([[a]])^2 + ([[b]])^2} = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This adds the coefficients' magnitudes; the amplitude is \( \sqrt{a^2+b^2} \), not \( |a|+|b| \).
This forgets to take the square root of \( a^2+b^2 \).
This subtracts the squares instead of adding them under the root.
This takes only the \( \sin \) coefficient as the amplitude.
This takes only the \( \cos \) coefficient as the amplitude.
This is off by one from the correct amplitude.

stem: What is the minimum value of \( ([[a]])\sin x + ([[b]])\cos x \)?
solution: Rewrite \( a\sin x + b\cos x = R\sin(x+\varphi) \) with \( R = \sqrt{a^2+b^2} \). Since \( \sin(x+\varphi) \) ranges over \( [-1,1] \), the minimum is \( -R = -\sqrt{([[a]])^2 + ([[b]])^2} = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This adds the coefficients' magnitudes; the amplitude is \( \sqrt{a^2+b^2} \), not \( |a|+|b| \).
This has the wrong sign; the minimum is the negative of the amplitude.
This forgets to take the square root of \( a^2+b^2 \).
This takes only the \( \sin \) coefficient's magnitude as the amplitude.
This takes only the \( \cos \) coefficient's magnitude as the amplitude.
This is off by one from the correct minimum.

stem: What is the maximum value of \( ([[a]])\sin x + ([[b]])\cos x + ([[d]]) \)?
solution: Since \( a\sin x+b\cos x \) alone has amplitude \( R=\sqrt{a^2+b^2} \) and ranges over \( [-R,R] \), adding the constant \( d \) shifts the whole range to \( [d-R,\,d+R] \). The maximum is \( R+d = \sqrt{([[a]])^2+([[b]])^2} + ([[d]]) = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This forgets to add the vertical shift \( d \) at all.
This mistakenly folds \( d \) into the \( \sin \) coefficient before computing the amplitude.
This uses \( |a|+|b| \) instead of \( \sqrt{a^2+b^2} \) for the amplitude.
This subtracts \( d \) instead of adding it.
This has the amplitude and shift in the wrong order/sign.
This double-counts the vertical shift.

stem: For what positive value of \( c \) does \( ([[a]])\sin x + c\cos x \) have a maximum value of \( [[M]] \)?
solution: The maximum of \( a\sin x + c\cos x \) is \( \sqrt{a^2+c^2} \). Setting \( \sqrt{([[a]])^2+c^2} = [[M]] \) and solving for the positive root gives \( c = \sqrt{[[M]]^2 - ([[a]])^2} = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This subtracts \( a \) from \( M \) directly instead of using the Pythagorean relationship.
This forgets to square \( M \) and \( a \) before subtracting under the root.
This stops at \( M^2-a^2 \) without taking the square root.
This adds the squares instead of subtracting.
This is off by one from the correct value.
This is off by one from the correct value.
